name: DOE-Prompt-ScientificObjectives
description: DOE Office of Science Scientific Objectives section - research goals, significance, knowledge gaps, connection to DOE mission

variants.name: default
variants.text:
You are writing the SCIENTIFIC OBJECTIVES section of a U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) Office of Science grant proposal. INPUTS: - {{content}}: The researcher's actual work, publications, and technical expertise. This is your PRIMARY source for all scientific content, prior results, and technical innovations. - {{grant_info}}: The specific DOE funding opportunity description, including program office (BES, BER, HEP, NP, FES, ASCR, etc.), priority research directions, and requirements. YOUR TASK: Generate ONLY the Scientific Objectives section. Do NOT generate the Technical Approach, Budget, Statement of Work, or any other proposal component. Those are handled separately. NOTE: These prompts are a best-effort starting point. The researcher should review and update all content to reflect their specific work, institutional context, and the latest program priorities. SECTION STRUCTURE: 1. OVERVIEW AND SCIENTIFIC MOTIVATION - State the central scientific problem in 2-3 paragraphs - Explain the fundamental knowledge gap this research addresses - Connect explicitly to the DOE Office of Science mission: advancing the scientific foundations for energy technologies, environmental stewardship, and national security - Reference the specific program office and priority research directions from {{grant_info}} 2. SPECIFIC SCIENTIFIC OBJECTIVES - State 3-5 specific, measurable scientific objectives derived from {{content}} - For each objective: * What will be accomplished * Why it is scientifically significant * How it connects to DOE mission priorities in {{grant_info}} * What new fundamental understanding it will produce - Objectives should be ambitious but achievable within the proposed period of performance 3. RELATIONSHIP TO DOE PRIORITY RESEARCH DIRECTIONS - Identify which Priority Research Directions (PRDs) or Basic Research Needs (BRNs) from {{grant_info}} this work addresses - Explain how the proposed research advances those priorities - If the work spans multiple program areas, identify the primary program and note secondary connections - Use the exact terminology from {{grant_info}} when describing alignment 4. SIGNIFICANCE AND IMPACT ON FUNDAMENTAL SCIENCE - Explain what new scientific knowledge will be produced - Describe how this advances understanding in the field beyond the current state of knowledge - Identify connections to other scientific disciplines where relevant - Distinguish between near-term scientific outcomes and longer-term potential impacts on energy technologies or national priorities 5. RELATIONSHIP TO PRIOR WORK - Summarize the current state of knowledge relevant to each objective - Reference the researcher's prior work from {{content}} as the foundation - Identify where current understanding is incomplete or where existing approaches have limitations - Position this proposal as the logical next step - Do NOT fabricate citations — use works from {{content}} or use phrases like "recent work has shown..." without inventing sources WRITING GUIDELINES: - Write for expert peer reviewers who are specialists in the relevant DOE program area - Use precise technical terminology from {{content}} - Do NOT reframe the research as AI/ML unless {{content}} explicitly describes AI/ML as core methodology - Every scientific claim should be traceable to {{content}} or clearly labeled as a proposed hypothesis - Do NOT fabricate preliminary data or citations not in {{content}} - Emphasize fundamental science — DOE Office of Science funds basic research, not applied development - If {{content}} does not provide sufficient detail for a subsection, note what additional information would strengthen it DOE REVIEW CRITERIA TO ADDRESS: - Scientific or technical merit of the proposed research - Appropriateness of the proposed method or approach - Competency of the applicant's personnel and adequacy of proposed resources - Reasonableness and appropriateness of the proposed budget - Relevance to the program mission and priorities OUTPUT FORMAT: Generate well-structured HTML: - Use

for the section title "Scientific Objectives" - Use

for each major subsection - Use

for individual objectives - Use

for paragraph content - Use

    /
      and
    1. for lists - Use for emphasis sparingly - Target approximately 4-6 pages (2,500-4,000 words)
